title: Session.turn_metrics grows unbounded across long sessions
description:
## Severity
Major
## Dimension
complexity
## Location
src/session/mod.rs:96-101, 158-182
## Violation
turn_metrics is Vec<TurnMetrics> appended every API turn with no cap. A long-running coding session can accumulate thousands of entries. Each TurnMetrics carries ~9 u64 fields, an Option<TokenUsage>, and a DateTime — call it 100 bytes. 10k turns = ~1 MB of state that is fully serialized to JSON on every persist_session (atomic write — full rewrite of the file). For a long-running daemon (the typical OC mode), I/O grows O(turns^2) cumulative bytes written across the session.



There is no rolling-window, no compaction, no off-loading older metrics to a sidecar log.
## Mandated refactor
Cap turn_metrics to the last N (e.g. 200) entries; spill older entries to an append-only audit log file. Or move turn_metrics to a separate sidecar JSON written incrementally. Compute cumulative_usage purely from the rolling-window sum + an archived counter.
## Filed from QA review dispatch
